title: Trump’s ceasefire ‘over’ remarks & new Hormuz disruptions: What it means for India
content: Before the conflict, the Strait of Hormuz handled nearly 40% of India's crude oil imports, around 60% of its LNG imports and approximately 90% of its LPG supplies, underlining the country's heavy dependence on the strategic waterway.

title: 'I'm on every single one of their lists': Trump claims Iran is plotting to kill him
content: President Trump stated Iran plots his assassination and US strikes Iran.  American forces targeted Iranian air defense systems and radar installations.  Iran launched drone and missile attacks on US military facilities.  Kuwaiti authorities intercepted multiple ballistic missiles and drones launched by Iran.  This escalation follows a similar exchange late last month.

title: Marco Rubio announces system for passport verification using phone, laptop camera
content: Americans will soon use smartphone cameras for passport photos, eliminating photo center visits. The US State Department is developing technology for nearly complete online passport applications. Facial recognition will verify images captured directly on personal devices. Future passports will include presentation boxes and historical QR codes. These changes aim to make obtaining a passport faster and more convenient.

title: Mamata Banerjee loses cool, slaps TMC worker during protest; BJP shares video
content: A video showed Mamata Banerjee slapping a party worker during a protest march in Kolkata. The incident occurred during a rally organized by the TMC's youth wing. Clashes erupted between BJP and TMC supporters along the protest route. Mamata Banerjee accused the police of failing to enforce court orders. The protest was held amid outrage over a minor girl's alleged rape and murder.

title: Big blow for India: Opener ruled out of historic Lord's Test vs England; here's why
content: Head coach Amol Muzumdar said Rawal picked up the injury while playing for India A against England A in Taunton earlier this month. She suffered a cut on her knee during India A's second 50-over match, which required stitches.

title: New USCIS rules for H-1B, Green Card applicants start July 10
content: New United States Citizenship and Immigration Services rules will take effect soon. Applicants must use handwritten or properly reproduced signatures for immigration forms. Improper signatures will lead to rejection without any chance for correction. Applicants should always sign forms by hand before submitting them. This policy aims to maintain the integrity of the immigration system.

title: 'Return to dialogue': India urges restraint after ships attacked amid uncertainty over Iran-US deal
content: India has expressed significant worry regarding the rising tensions in West Asia and the increasing assaults on commercial vessels. The ministry emphasized the importance of all involved parties to practice restraint and engage in dialogue. Recent actions pose risks to regional stability and disrupt global trade. In response, the United States has executed extensive military strikes against Iranian targets, while Iran retaliated with strikes aimed at Bahrain and Kuwait.

title: 200-foot trees may survive drought better than scientists thought
content: Recent studies unveil that massive trees in Southeast Asia possess remarkable adaptations in their water transport mechanisms. These trees, known for reaching impressive heights, can effectively draw water upward, defying the challenges posed by gravity. This groundbreaking research, focusing on Malaysia's dipterocarps, shows that taller trees feature broader xylem vessels, offering fresh insights into their resilience amidst climate change.

title: Scientists turn shrimp shell waste into carbon-negative hydrogen fuel
content: Researchers have pioneered a groundbreaking method that transforms organic waste into hydrogen fuel, while simultaneously generating protein suitable for aquaculture feed and calcium carbonate. This innovative technology aims to achieve a carbon-neutral process, capturing more carbon dioxide than it emits. Ongoing investigations are focused on potential commercial uses for this adaptable technology, though scaling up remains a notable hurdle to achieving widespread use.

title: Cursor field CTO says it is 'goodbye time' for Jeff Bezos 'pizza team meeting rule'
content: Jeff Bezos' famous two-pizza rule for team size is now being questioned in the AI era. David Pan believes artificial intelligence tools allow for even smaller and more efficient engineering teams. This idea has sparked debate online, with some suggesting new metaphors for team size. However, others still support the original two-pizza principle for team management. Meanwhile, SpaceX recently acquired an AI coding startup named Cursor for sixty billion dollars.

title: US stocks: Oil jumps nearly 5%, shares tumble as Trump declares Iran ceasefire 'over'
content: Global stock markets declined and oil prices surged on Wednesday. US President Donald Trump cast doubt on the temporary Iran conflict truce. This raised fears that renewed fighting could disrupt global energy supplies. Wall Street opened lower after Trump suggested the ceasefire was effectively over. Investors are concerned that renewed hostilities could disrupt shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Connor Murphy dead or alive? Viral YouTube bodybuilder death rumor explained
content: Connor Murphy’s reported death remains an unconfirmed rumor as of July 2026. Claims circulating online largely stem from social media posts and statements that have not been independently verified. No family member, representative, or authority has confirmed his death. Murphy rose to fame through fitness and bodybuilding content, and questions about his current status remain unanswered pending official information.

title: Nolan Wells update: Family hires Ben Crump after Horn Island tragedy
content: Nolan Xavier Wells, an 18-year-old Mississippi student-athlete, was found dead on Horn Island after going missing during a Fourth of July trip with friends. Authorities have not announced a cause of death and say the investigation is ongoing. His family has hired attorney Ben Crump and is calling for a thorough, transparent review of the circumstances surrounding his death.

title: Who are Arthur Fery's parents? Meet Loïc Fery and former pro Olivia Fery
content: British tennis player Arthur Fery has become one of the home favourites at Wimbledon thanks to his inspiring journey and strong connection with the tournament. Having grown up close to the All England Club, the 23-year-old has always dreamed of competing on the famous grass courts in front of British fans.

title: 'They may target again': Dombivli doctor assaulted by Sena corporator weighs quitting job
content: A young doctor is traumatized after an assault at Shastri Nagar Hospital.  She is now considering quitting her job despite the main accused's arrest.  Her family expressed concerns about her safety and mental well-being following the incident.  The attack on medical staff led to widespread outrage and protests by doctors.  Routine OPD services were suspended while emergency care continued during the protest.

title: Meta launches AI image models, company's 'highest-paid employee' shares a 'personal photo' tip
content: Meta launched Muse Image, its first AI image generator from Meta Superintelligence Labs, alongside a preview of Muse Video. Chief AI officer Alexandr Wang, one of the company's highest-paid hires, urged users to keep it personal: upload your own photos, tag friends, and pull from trends. The launch also drew privacy backlash over a feature that uses public Instagram photos by default.

title: Gaurav Khanna special guest entry: Actor to join Lock Upp house
content: Akanksha Chamola revealed her separation from Gaurav Khanna on Lock Upp. She stated they are heading towards a mutual divorce after one year apart. Gaurav Khanna will now enter the Lock Upp as a special guest. His appearance marks his first house entry since Akanksha's participation. Viewers remain confused about their relationship's current status.

title: Mystery 'space balls' wash ashore on Australian beach; scientists explain
content: Metallic spheres have mysteriously appeared on an Australian beach, sparking local alarm. After thorough investigation, authorities determined the oddities were actually remnants of space debris—specifically pressure vessels from rocket launch vehicles that survived their fiery return to Earth. With the surge in space explorations, experts predict that encounters with such debris will become more frequent as launches increase.

title: Watch: Gaganyaan parachute system in action as Isro tests crew module’s safe splashdown
content: ISRO successfully conducted a key qualification test for Gaganyaan's main parachute system. This test validated the parachute's structural integrity under maximum expected load conditions. A simulated assembly was dropped from an aircraft to test the system's performance. This marks the fifth integrated main parachute airdrop test for the mission. The successful test provides confidence in the parachute system for the upcoming uncrewed mission.

title: Watch: Pinaka long-range rocket roars off launcher in DRDO’s latest test
content: The Defence Research and Development Organisation successfully conducted a flight-test of the Pinaka Long Range Guided Rocket. This successful trial marks another milestone in India's indigenous rocket artillery development. The system is designed to deliver precision strikes at extended ranges for the Army. This latest test follows a successful proof trial of the Pinaka Extended Range Rocket.

title: What's 1994 'robbery' reference in Argentina's WC anthem Messi sings - Explained
content: Argentina looked on the verge of elimination after falling 2-0 behind in the 67th minute. But the defending champions scored three goals in the final 13 minutes, with Cristian Romero, Lionel Messi and Enzo Fernandez finding the net to complete the turnaround.

title: Baruipur rape-murder probe: Accused sold 12-yr-old to co-accused for Rs 10,000
content: Police encounter death of Prabhas Mondal will not derail the investigation. Mondal confessed to luring the child for Rs 10,000 cash. His disclosures led to the victim's body recovery and other arrests. Forensic evidence and CCTV footage have already been secured. The investigation will continue against the three surviving accused.

title: China will benefit if India-US trade deal doesn’t happen: Economist Surjit Bhalla
content: The trade deal has the potential to boost competitiveness and accelerate India’s journey towards becoming a developed nation, Surjit Bhalla said. However, he warned that prolonged delays in concluding such an agreement would ultimately work to China's advantage.

title: 'Honeymoon suite' inside Indian Railways 1st AC coach goes viral; TTE suspended
content: A viral video showed an Indian Railways First AC cabin transformed into a honeymoon suite. The compartment was decorated with balloons and floral arrangements for a couple. Railway authorities suspended the Traveling Ticket Examiner at Jalna station. An official inquiry has been ordered into the incident which occurred on July 6. The event styling service claimed ignorance about needing official permission.

title: Fans react as Wimbledon celebrates Novak Djokovic and Lionel Messi's legacy
content: Novak Djokovic delivered one of the toughest wins of his career. He survived a five-hour-15-minute battle against Felix Auger-Aliassime in the Wimbledon 2026 quarter-finals. The Serbian star fought through a calf injury and immense pressure to secure a dramatic five-set victory. His remarkable effort booked a blockbuster semi-final clash against world No. 1 Jannik Sinner.

title: Chennai GM Open: Full player list from Gukesh to Niemann, format, prize money, and key details
content: The Chennai Grand Masters 2026 will be held from July 16-22 in Chennai, featuring World Champion Gukesh Dommaraju, Arjun Erigaisi, Hans Niemann and other top grandmasters. The eight-player single round-robin tournament offers a Rs 75 lakh prize pool and crucial FIDE Circuit points. Organisers have confirmed there will be no Challengers section this year.

title: 'Deeply saddened': Jay Shah mourns passing of ex-Afghanistan cricketer Zadran
content: Former Afghanistan fast bowler Shapoor Zadran has died aged 38 after battling the rare immune disorder HLH. The Afghanistan Cricket Board and ICC Chairman Jay Shah paid tribute, hailing him as a pioneer of Afghan cricket. Shah remembered Zadran's match-winning heroics in Afghanistan's first-ever World Cup victory in 2015. He played 80 international matches between 2009 and 2020.

title: 'Asked to stay away for a few days': Gopal Rao denies being removed from Ram Temple trust
content: Gopal Rao stated he was asked to temporarily stay away from trust meetings.  Champat Rai and Anil Mishra resigned from the trust taking moral responsibility.  Most allegations against Rao are false, except for donation theft claims.  Donations are safe and verifiable, with police handling theft arrests.  An SIT continues its investigation into alleged donation misappropriation.

title: Mamata rally turns tense: 'Chor chor' slogans raised, eggs thrown, BJP-TMC workers clash
content: Eggs were allegedly thrown at Mamata Banerjee's protest march in Kolkata on Wednesday. Clashes broke out between Trinamool Congress and BJP workers during the rally. Police resorted to a baton charge to control the escalating situation and restore order. Mamata Banerjee accused the police of acting as an arm of the BJP organization. The Calcutta High Court had previously allowed the protest march under specific conditions.

title: Israeli strike kills Gaza's Argentina-Egypt FIFA WC screening organiser
content: The strike came just before what was expected to be a major occasion for football fans in Gaza, where many had gathered to watch Egypt face defending champions Argentina.

title: Nato chief defends US strikes on Iran, calls them 'absolutely necessary'
content: Nato Secretary General Mark Rutte defended recent US military strikes on Iran. He described the action as absolutely necessary after alleged Iranian attacks on commercial vessels. US forces targeted over eighty sites in response to the maritime incidents. Iran retaliated with drone and missile attacks on US-linked facilities. The events occurred ahead of the 2026 Nato Summit in Ankara.

title: US-Iran war impact: IMF lowers India’s GDP growth forecast marginally
content: According to the IMF, the global economy has so far avoided a steeper slowdown despite the war, as strong demand linked to the technology sector has helped offset the impact of reduced energy supplies caused by the conflict.

title: Building collapses in Delhi's Rohini after rain; several feared trapped
content: An under-construction house collapsed in northwest Delhi's Rohini on Wednesday evening. Rainfall preceded the incident, prompting an immediate rescue operation. Two individuals have been pulled from the debris so far. Rescue efforts are still underway near an MCD school in Sector 16. Police and other emergency teams have cordoned off the affected area.

title: Egypt lodge complaint with FIFA after Argentina loss, seek referee's removal
content: The Egyptian camp was unhappy with several refereeing decisions during the match. These included a goal ruled out after a VAR review and a penalty appeal that was not reviewed in the build-up to Argentina's winning goal.

title: Shiv Sena corporator arrested for assault on doctor, hospital staff in Thane
content: In Thane, Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre faces arrest following an incident where medical staff were assaulted. The altercation erupted when the relatives of a newborn expressed frustration at the hospital. In response, doctors across various hospitals have suspended outpatient services, demanding the apprehension of all involved. Though Mhatre expressed sorrow over the incident, he refuted claims of having assaulted a female doctor.

title: 'I can't look at you, I'm sorry': Argentina coach's post-match interview goes viral - Watch
content: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni broke down in tears after his side's dramatic 3-2 World Cup comeback win over Egypt, walking away from a live TV interview after apologising. Argentina overturned a 2-0 deficit with late goals from Cristian Romero, Lionel Messi and Enzo Fernández. Scaloni later said, "I cried because I'm too emotional," praising his team's fighting spirit.

title: Kumarakom to get solar-powered tourist boat services
content: The initiative, expected to be launched from Muhamma, will enable tourists to travel to popular destinations such as Pathiramanal, Kayippuram and Thanneermukkam, among other

title: Narasaraopet to host ₹2,000 crore mega loan mela on July 17: MP
content: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman and Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu are expected to attend the programme, which aims to benefit about 3,000 people under around 20 Central government schemes

title: Wayanad debris slip highlights: CM announces ex gratia of ₹5 lakh to families of killed; says priority is to trace missing people
content: A day after a debris slip at the tunnel road construction site in Kerala's Wayanad district claimed three lives, extensive search operations are ongoing to find five people who are still missing

title: Amit Shah orders third-party audit to cut airport passenger delays
content: Shah directed the phased installation of Automatic Tray Retrieval System at airports across the country, to ease the congestion at security checkpoints and reduce manpower requirements

title: Supreme Court seeks government response on plea by Gulf students that ‘assessment scheme’ hurts prospects
content: Students say the assessment scheme ‘drastically lowered their marks and jeopardised’ their higher education prospects

title: Besant Road clears up after hawker eviction
content: Traffic eases after unlicensed vendors are removed following the High Court’s directions; shopkeepers welcome the move while displaced hawkers fear loss of livelihood

title: Tihar to respond to terror-accused U.S. national’s plea for ‘American-style diet’
content: Request made purely on humanitarian considerations and intended to safeguard the health of their client while in judicial custody, counsel for the accused says

title: Seven rescued after building collapses in Pune district, nine still trapped
content: The structure collapsed after a huge mound of legacy waste piled on the premises came loose during heavy rain and hit the building, said the corporation’s commissioner

title: NCW seeks report on gangrape of minor girl in Sri Ganganagar within 15 days
content: The commission said that such an incident reflects point to administrative lapses, policing gaps, and inadequate monitoring mechanisms that allowed such criminal activities to continue and sought a crackdown against illegal hotels

title: CAG report slams delay, cost escalation in Kochi Corporation headquarters project
content: Project cost rose from ₹18.83 crore to around ₹61 crore; audit also found unresolved basement waterlogging and pending works

title: Kerala’s Thiruvonam bumper lottery first prize hiked to ₹30 crore
content: Ticket price remains unchanged at ₹500 per ticket. The third prize revised from र50 lakh to 25 lakh as part of prize structure revision

title: Calcutta High Court seeks affidavit as State says no decision yet on midday meal contract for ISKCON
content: The announcement to engage ISKCON was made in the State Budget presented by Finance Minister Swapan Dasgupta on June 22, drawing criticism from Opposition parties who claim it is an attempt by BJP government to impose vegetarianism

title: PF interest to be credited by July 15 to member accounts: Minister
content: Union Labour Minister Mansukh Mandaviya says claims will now undergo automated pre-validation prior to processing at EPFO offices

title: U.S. students study natural farming practices in Kuppam
content: Academic tour under the APCNF programme gives students first-hand exposure to sustainable farming methods and interactions with cultivators

title: Violence at Trinamool rally in Kolkata; Mamata accuses BJP government of allowing lawlessness
content: Former CM claims police failed to prevent attacks on Trinamool workers during protest against Baruipur minor rape and murder case

title: No evidence via ‘Op. Hard Ball’ investigation links Indian Govt. to Nijjar killing: Canada Police
content: "There's no evidence to suggest through this organised crime investigation and the charges and the indictment laid forward that Indian officials were charged or involved," a senior Canadian police official said

title: Kerala High Court allows 'last chance' to investigating officer to complete probe in SNDP microfinance scam
content: Kerala government informs the court that 'bona fide attempts' were made by the investigating officer and that he needed one more month to complete the investigation into the alleged misappropriation

title: Punjab Police officer among 37 wanted by U.S. along with gangsters Lawrence Bishnoi, Bhagwanpuria
content: The police officer is accused of working closely with Jaggu Bhagwanpuria, 38, a gangster imprisoned in India

title: Greater Bengaluru Authority to take up citywide crackdown on abandoned vehicles occupying public space from July 10
content: The five city corporations under the Greater Bengaluru Authority will launch a joint drive with the Bengaluru Traffic Police to remove abandoned vehicles from roads and public spaces from July 10

title: Wayanad debris slip: No breakthrough in search for five missing persons
content: Satheesan announces an ex gratia of ₹5 lakh to the families of those killed; the government will bear the treatment cost of the injured

title: Blame game over waste management in Thiruvananthapuram
content: Mayor alleges that as the BJP has over the past six months of its stint in the Corporation earned a good impression from general public, CPI(M) leaders are attempting to dump waste all around the city and spread fake news. LDF says Mayor is making contradictory statements instead of giving clear answers to questions raised by people, Opposition and media

title: 676 Assam govt. schools without electricity, 238 lack boys’ toilets
content: Education Minister Ranoj Pegu says the State seeks to provide all essential infrastructure by March 2027

title: ED freezes ₹440 crore in different bank accounts of Trinamool Congress
content: Both the factions of Trinamool Congress, one led by ex-CM Mamata Banerjee and another led by Leader of Opposition Ritabrata Banerjee, have claimed ownership over the accounts

title: Delhi, Mumbai grapple with heavy rain havoc | Monsoon LIVE updates
content: Three-storey under-construction house in Delhi's Rohini collapsed on Wednesday evening, trapping several people; 16 people are feared to be trapped under a collapsed garbage mound in Maharashtra's Pimpri Chinchwad

title: India’s higher education enrolment hits 4.5 crore; women, marginalised groups lead way: AISHE report
content: Female educators are increasingly representing the academic workforce, with their numbers rising to 7.78 lakh, accounting for 44.9% of the total teaching strength, the report noted

title: TTD suspends VIP break darshan on key festival days
content: VIP recommendation letters will not be accepted on the day preceding each of the notified dates, says TTD

title: Karnataka Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar extends support to Operation Toofan; meeting of south India DGPs on July 10
content: Ramesh Chennithala meets Chief Minister. Responding to Chennithala’s request for stricter surveillance along the Kerala-Karnataka border, Shivakumar assures that additional police personnel will be deployed to strengthen inspections and enforcement activities in border areas

title: ED searches money changers’ premises over alleged FEMA violations
content: The agency alleged that foreign currency was being exchanged outside the licensed framework without adherence to KYC requirements, prescribed documentation, and invoicing procedures

title: Four teachers suspended for ‘lapses’ during Amarnath yatra; suspicious youths tagged by face recognition held
content: Officials said the Ganderbal administration was keeping a close eye on the process of on-spot registration of pilgrims heading for the Amarnath yatra from the Baltal route

title: Sakhi centres offer vital support to women in distress: Women’s Commission Chairperson
content: Sailaja says trauma-informed care and staff well-being are essential for delivering sensitive, survivor-centred services

title: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu directs tobacco firms to stick to procurement commitments, expedite auction process
content: Expressing displeasure at the slow pace of procurement, he asks the companies to ensure purchases at, or above, the minimum support price of ₹200 per kg fixed by the State government; crisis largely due to farmers cultivating beyond the demand projected through indent allocations, argue companies

title: DRDO successfully tests Pinaka long-range guided rocket
content: The Pinaka rocket executed all planned in-flight manoeuvres, and hit the designated target with high precision, following the predicted trajectory, the Union Ministry of Defence says

title: Landslides will continue unless there is accountability, say scientists, environmentalists
content: They point out that the recent episodes are a result of a combination of geological factors, meteorological and topographical changes

title: Monsoon In Bursts: Why Several States Are Flooded In 'Below-Normal' Year
content: The first eight days of July brought a sharp increase in rainfall across several parts of the country.

title: In Talks, Myanmar Assures Its Land Will Not Be Used For Action Against India
content: An important assurance given by the country to India is that no action will be undertaken against the interests of India from Myanmar territory.

title: Sena Corporator Who Assaulted Doctors Admitted To Hospital After Arrest
content: Deputy Commissioner of Police (DCP) Atul Zende of Kalyan said Mhatre was taken into custody in connection with the criminal case registered against him and his aides at Vishnunagar police station.

title: Water In Mumbai's Lakes Crosses 41%, But 10% Cut To Continue
content: The current water stock stands at 5.98 lakh million litres, enough to meet Mumbai's water requirements until mid-October, officials said.

title: 'Exercise Restraint, Return To Diplomacy': India On Fresh Middle-East Tensions
content: Following the strikes reportedly by Iran, the US attacked a number of Iranian military installations. Iran also fired missiles on Kuwait and Bahrain.

title: "Serious Blot On Civilised Society": Bombay High Court On Manual Scavenging
content: The Aurangabad bench of the high court held the state responsible for ensuring Rs 30 lakh compensation reaches the victims' families.

title: Smart Security, Fast Immigration: Centre Plans Key Airport Upgrades
content: Among the biggest announcements was the decision to roll out the Automatic Tray Retrieval System (ATRS) in a phased manner across airports nationwide.

title: 1 Killed, Several Feared Trapped As Under-Construction Building Collapses In Delhi
content: Two people were pulled out of the rubble as police, NDRF, and other emergency teams rushed to the site.

title: Husband Stabs Wife To Death On Road Near Kolkata, Then Attempts Suicide
content: The victim has been identified as Gita Das, while the accused is Subhash Das, a resident of Nazat in North 24 Parganas.

title: Tamil Nadu's Construction Industry Says Excavation Curbs Stalled Projects
content: According to CREDAI, the disruption has delayed project execution and completion timelines, exposing developers to possible violations of commitments.

title: "Highlights Inhuman Torture, Sacrifice": Sukhbir Badal Says Will Screen "Satluj"
content: Sukhbir Badal said Satluj "narrates the truth of that painful period which did not stop even after the Congress party's military attack on Sachkhand Sri Harmandir Sahib and the burning alive of thousands of unarmed innocent Sikh youth"

title: Pak-Linked Plot To Target Delhi Markets, Bus Stand Foiled, 6 Arrested: Cops
content: The New Police Lines in Civil Lines, Anand Vihar Bus Terminal and the railway station, and several crowded markets were on the terrorists' target list, the Delhi Police said.

title: Child Marriage Ban Applies To All Religions: Allahabad High Court
content: It made clear that no personal law, including Muslim personal law, can violate the Prohibition of Child Marriage Act or bypass the POCSO Act.

title: Iran Carries Out Retaliatory Strikes On US Military Bases In Bahrain, Kuwait
content: The United States launched extensive strikes on Iran over attacks on ships in the vital Strait of Hormuz, triggering a wave of reprisals Wednesday that Iran's Guards said...

title: Bigger Disaster Still Looms: Experts' Warning After Wayanad Landslide
content: Public Works Minister PK Basheer has since confirmed that repeated directions were issued to remove the accumulated soil.

title: Why PM Modi's Indonesia-Australia Tour Is Crucial For India's EV Ambitions
content: Lithium and nickel have thus become even more important to make India self-reliant in pushing out indigenous EV vehicles.

title: Video: Mamata Banerjee Slaps Man Amid Trinamool-BJP Clash At Kolkata Rally
content: The Trinamool Congress rally was regularly interrupted by jostling, pushing, and shoving between workers of the party and the BJP.

title: 'Been Lucky, Maybe That Won't Last': Trump Claims Iran Plotting To Kill Him
content: Addressing reporters on the sidelines of the NATO Summit in Turkey, Trump said that although he has been "lucky" all this while, when it comes to assassinations, it might not continue to be the case.

title: Desi doctor who drove his family off a cliff in California walks free after therapy
content: the 45-year-old radiologist lived with his wife and two children in a quiet cul-de-sac in Pasadena, California . prosecutors say he deliberately drove his Tesla off a 250-foot cliff at Devil's Slide, a notorious stretch of California's Pacific Coast Highway . all four survived, and a judge dismissed all three attempted murder charges .
